extra.message: User-controlled 'code' parameter from registration form is directly interpolated into a raw SQL string via f-string passed to sqlalchemy.text() and session.execute(). This allows SQL injection to read, modify, or delete arbitrary database records.

User-controlled 'search' query parameter from request.args.get('search', '') is directly interpolated into a raw SQL LIKE clause via f-string passed to sqlalchemy.text(). This allows SQL injection on the notes table.

User-controlled request.path is concatenated into an f-string that is passed to render_template_string(). This allows Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI), enabling arbitrary Jinja2 expression evaluation which can lead to remote code execution via access to Flask internals and Python built-ins.

User-controlled 'preferences' cookie is base64-decoded and deserialized using pickle.loads() on every request. Pickle deserialization of untrusted data allows arbitrary code execution by crafting a malicious pickle payload.

User-controlled URL from the 'url' field in the profile image form (routes/account.py:60-61) is passed to urllib.request.urlopen() without any validation or restrictions. This allows Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF), enabling the attacker to make requests to internal services, cloud metadata endpoints, or arbitrary external hosts.

The /account POST route is missing the @login_required decorator and blindly applies all form fields (including 'is_admin') to the current_user object via current_user.__dict__.update(filtered_values). An authenticated non-admin user can escalate privileges to admin by submitting is_admin=True in the POST body.

The /accounts/<int:user_id>/notes endpoint queries notes filtered by the user-supplied user_id parameter without verifying that the authenticated user owns those notes. Any authenticated user can view any other user's private notes by changing the user_id in the URL (IDOR).

cwe: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key

extra.message: The /notes/<int:note_id>/delete endpoint deletes any note by ID without verifying that the note belongs to the current user. An authenticated attacker can delete arbitrary notes belonging to other users by sending a POST request with a different note_id.

cwe: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key

extra.message: The note.text field is rendered with the |safe Jinja2 filter, disabling HTML auto-escaping. Since note content is user-controlled (via the /notes POST endpoint) and stored in the database, this allows stored Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) attacks against any user viewing notes on the home page.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation

extra.message: The table rendering uses safe_columns=['text'] which disables HTML escaping for the 'text' column. Note content is user-controlled and stored in the database. When a victim views search results, any HTML/JavaScript in note.text will execute in their browser (stored XSS).

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation

extra.message: The Flask secret_key is hardcoded as 'super secret key', a weak and predictable value. This allows session forgery and tampering, as an attacker can forge session cookies to impersonate any user.

All forms in the application extend wtforms.Form instead of FlaskForm (from Flask-WTF), which means no CSRF tokens are generated or validated. All POST endpoints (/login, /signup, /notes, /account, /account/image, /darkmode, /registration-codes, /notes/<id>/delete) are vulnerable to Cross-Site Request Forgery attacks.

The 404 error handler passes the full error object into render_template_string and renders it to the user. Error objects typically contain exception messages, stack traces, and internal application state which can aid attackers in further exploitation.

cwe: CWE-209: Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information

extra.message: The /darkmode POST route is missing the @login_required decorator, allowing unauthenticated access. While the darkmode toggle itself is low-severity, this route handles pickle-serialized data from cookies, which combined with the insecure deserialization vulnerability expands the attack surface.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function
